mnemonics.title: Queue Operations Order
mnemonics.content: FARE - Front Always Removed, Enter at Rear. Remember: In queue, you enter at the BACK (rear) and leave from the FRONT.

mnemonics.title: Front and Rear Pointers
mnemonics.content: FROnt points to the FIrst element (where removal happens), REAr points to the REcent element (where insertion happens).

mnemonics.title: Empty vs Full Queue
mnemonics.content: E.F. - Empty when Front > Rear (or both -1), Full when Rear = MAX-1. Think: 'ER' for Empty Rear = -1.

mnemonics.title: Circular Queue Solution
mnemonics.content: In C-Queue, C wraps around - think of the array as a CIRCLE, not a line. When rear reaches end, it WRAPS to beginning if space available.

acronyms.usage: The fundamental principle of queue - first element inserted is first element removed
Operation to add an element to the back of the queue
Operation to remove an element from the front of the queue

acronyms.expansion: REAR points to position for next ENTRY
acronyms.usage: The index where new elements are added

acronyms.expansion: FRONT points to element available for removal
acronyms.usage: The index from which elements are removed

visualTips.visualization: Imagine people standing in a checkout line. New people join at the END (rear), and the person at the FRONT gets served first and leaves. The line grows and shrinks but always processes from front.

Think of front and rear as two 'pointers' sliding along a track. Front slides right when you remove, rear slides right when you add. In linear queue, they never go back - causing waste. In circular, they wrap around.
Draw a horizontal array. Initially mark both front and rear at position -1 (before the array starts). When you add first element, both pointers move to index 0.

analogies.analogy: Like a movie theater ticket line - new people join at the END, people get tickets and leave from the FRONT. The line (queue) maintains order.

Like a one-way street with cars. Once cars leave from front, those parking spaces are abandoned. Even if spaces at the beginning are empty, new cars cannot use them - causing traffic jam (overflow) even when street has space!
Think of front as the 'door' where people exit and rear as the 'entrance' where people join. They move forward as people go through, but never backward in linear queue.

Like a full parking lot - even though cars leave, new cars cannot enter until there's explicit space. In linear queue, it's like the entrance gate can only move forward, not back to reuse empty spots.
